reasoning: Based on the pattern from 'ye nihualla', keeping the same structure but converting to IPA notation
Converting 'antocnihuan in' - 'c' becomes 'k', 'h' is dropped based on observed patterns
Converting 'quin icuac' - 'qu' becomes 'k', 'c' before 'a' becomes 'k', following IPA patterns
Converting /weːweʔ/ - long vowel /eː/ becomes 'ē' with macron, glottal stop /ʔ/ is written as 'ʔ'
Converting /tʃokolaːtɬ/ - /tʃ/ becomes 'ch', /o/ becomes 'o', /k/ becomes 'c', long /aː/ becomes 'ā', /tɬ/ becomes 'tl'
Converting /miktɬaːnteːkʷtɬi/ - following patterns for consonants and marking long vowels with macrons

These two systems have a bijection because they represent the same information with only orthographic differences (macrons for long vowels, capitalization). The IPA contains additional phonetic information not present in either other system.
